saida_bruta:
As the boys came up the hill, she saw them hesitate and confer together. “You ask her, Niel.”

“You’d better, George. She goes to your house all the time, and she scarcely knows me well enough to speak to.”

When they stopped before the three steps leading to the front porch, Mrs. Forrester came to the door and nodded graciously, holding one of the pink roses in her hand.

“Good morning, boys. Are you off on a picnic?”

George Adams stepped forward and solemnly removed his large straw hat. “Good morning, Mrs. Forrester. May we fish and wade in the marsh, and have our lunch in the grove?”

“Certainly. You have a lovely day for it. How long has school been out? Don’t you miss it? I’m sure Niel does.

As the boys climbed the hill, she watched them stop and whisper together.

“You should ask, George. You’re always at her house, and she barely knows me well enough to talk to me.”

When they reached the three steps leading up to the porch, Mrs. Forrester appeared in the doorway. Holding a pink rose, she greeted them with a gracious nod.

“Good morning, boys. Are you off on a picnic?”

George Adams stepped forward and solemnly removed his broad straw hat. “Good morning, Mrs. Forrester. May we fish and wade in the marsh, then eat our lunch in the grove?”

“Of course. It’s a lovely day for it. How long has school been out? Don’t you miss it? I’m sure Niel does.”
